src: καὶ ἀδελφὸς ἐπὶ ἀδελφῆς,
lit: And brother upon sister,
sm: and brother against sister,

syn.mc: Coordinate nominal phrase continuing the description of social disorder.
syn.svo: No finite verb in this line; the phrase is a prepositional complement to the surrounding narrative.
syn.rhet: Paratactic accumulation of incestuous/violent inversions.

src: καὶ ἄρρην ἐπὶ ἄρρενος,
lit: And male upon male,
sm: and male against male,

syn.svo: No finite verb in this line; prepositional phrase continues the list of perversions.
syn.rhet: The series intensifies the inversion of natural relations.

src: καὶ ἕτερος ἐπὶ τὴν γυναῖκα τοῦ πλησίον ἐχρεμέτιζον.
lit: And another were neighing toward the wife of his neighbor.
sm: and another lusted after his neighbor's wife.
emd: The transmitted verb ἐχρεμέτιζον is not secure; I preserve it as a likely metaphor from χρεμετίζω, with an explicit uncertainty marker.

syn.mc: Main descriptive clause concluding the disorder catalogue.
syn.svo: Subject: ἕτερος. Verb: ἐχρεμέτιζον. Prepositional phrase: ἐπὶ τὴν γυναῖκα τοῦ πλησίον.
syn.rhet: Animalized sexual metaphor closes the sequence with ugly comic force.

src: ᾿Επὶ δὲ τούτοις ἡ ἁμαρτία ηὐφραίνετο,
ana: Ἐπὶ δὲ τούτοις ἡ ἁμαρτία ηὐφραίνετο,
lit: And over these things sin was rejoicing,
sm: And over these things sin rejoiced,
emd: Followed the general quotation-mark normalization policy and removed the stray spacing psili from ᾿Επὶ.

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; line 406 adds a participial expansion.
syn.svo: Subject: ἡ ἁμαρτία. Verb: ηὐφραίνετο. Prepositional phrase: ἐπὶ δὲ τούτοις.
syn.rhet: Sin is personified as gloating over disorder.

src: ἣ τοῦ θανάτου σύνεργος ὑπάρχουσα
tok: pres act ptcp nom sg f
lit: Which, being an ally of death,
sm: which, as an ally of death,

syn.svo: Subject of the clause: ἣ (= ἁμαρτία). Participial verb: ὑπάρχουσα. Predicate adjective: σύνεργος. Genitive complement: τοῦ θανάτου.
syn.rhet: Sin and death are welded together by personification and alliance language.

src: προοδοιπορεῖ εἰς τὰς τῶν ἀνθρώπων ψυχάς,
lit: She goes ahead into the souls of men,
sm: she goes before into human souls,

syn.mc: Main clause continuing the description of sin.
syn.svo: Subject: ἣ (= ἁμαρτία). Verb: προοδοιπορεῖ. Object/goal: εἰς τὰς ... ψυχάς.
syn.rhet: Sin is pictured as a forerunner into the interior life.

src: καὶ ἑτοιμάζει αὐτῷ τροφὰς τὰ τῶν νεκρῶν σώματα.
lit: And prepares for it food, the bodies of the dead,
sm: and prepares for it food, namely the bodies of the dead,

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the appositional phrase τὰ τῶν νεκρῶν σώματα explains τροφάς.
syn.svo: Subject: ἁμαρτία. Verb: ἑτοιμάζει. Indirect object: αὐτῷ. Object: τροφάς. Apposition/explanation: τὰ τῶν νεκρῶν σώματα.
syn.rhet: Grim personification turns corpses into sin's food.

src: εἰς πᾶσαν δὲ ψυχὴν ἐτίθει ἡ ἁμαρτία ἴχνος
lit: And into every soul sin was placing a track,
sm: And sin was placing a mark upon every soul,

syn.svo: Subject: ἡ ἁμαρτία. Verb: ἐτίθει. Object: ἴχνος. Prepositional phrase: εἰς πᾶσαν ψυχὴν.
syn.rhet: Sin is figured as leaving a visible track on each soul.

src: καὶ εἰς οὗς ἂν ἔθηκεν τούτους ἔδει τελευτᾶν.
lit: And those into whom it should have put these things had to die.
sm: And those into whom it set these marks had to die.

syn.mc: Main clause with embedded relative clause.
syn.sub: οὓς ἂν ἔθηκεν is a relative clause of general condition/indefinite reference.
syn.svo: Main clause: ἔδει τελευτᾶν. Subject of the relative clause: implied ἡ ἁμαρτία. Verb in relative clause: ἔθηκεν. Demonstrative object: τούτους.
syn.rhet: The mark of sin becomes a sentence of death.

src: πᾶσα οὖν σὰρξ ὑπὸ ἁμαρτίαν ἔπιπτεν
lit: Therefore all flesh was falling under sin
sm: Therefore all flesh was falling under sin,

syn.mc: Main clause concluding the causal sequence.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; οὖν draws the inference from the preceding causal chain.
syn.svo: Subject: πᾶσα σάρξ. Verb: ἔπιπτεν. Prepositional phrase: ὑπὸ ἁμαρτίαν.
syn.rhet: The universal scope of the problem is stated in a forceful generalization.

src: καὶ πᾶν σῶμα ὑπὸ θάνατον,
lit: And every body under death,
sm: and every body under death,

syn.sub: No finite verb is expressed; the verb ἔπιπτεν is carried over from line 411.
syn.svo: Subject: πᾶν σῶμα. Verb: ἔπιπτεν understood. Prepositional phrase: ὑπὸ θάνατον.
syn.rhet: The whole human condition is recast in parallel lines: sin / death.

src: καὶ πᾶσα ψυχὴ ἐκ τοῦ σαρκίνου οἴκου ἐξηλαύνετο.
lit: And every soul was being driven out from the fleshly house.
sm: and every soul was being driven out from the fleshly house.

syn.mc: Main clause continuing the anthropological lament.
syn.svo: Subject: πᾶσα ψυχή. Verb: ἐξηλαύνετο. Source phrase: ἐκ τοῦ σαρκίνου οἴκου.
syn.rhet: Body and soul are split by mortality.

src: καὶ τὸ λημφθὲν ἐκ γῆς εἰς γῆν ἀνελύετο,
tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg n
lit: And what had been taken from earth was being dissolved back into earth,
sm: and what was taken from earth was being dissolved back into earth,

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the participle λημφθὲν modifies the neuter subject.
syn.svo: Subject: τὸ λημφθέν. Verb: ἀνελύετο. Source and goal phrases: ἐκ γῆς εἰς γῆν.
syn.ptc: λημφθὲν is passive and antecedent, stressing origin.
syn.rhet: The formula of return to earth is stark and symmetrical.

src: καὶ τὸ δωρηθὲν ἐκ θεοῦ εἰς ἄδην κατεκλείετο"
ana: καὶ τὸ δωρηθὲν ἐκ θεοῦ εἰς ἄδην κατεκλείετο·
tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg n
lit: And what had been given from God was being shut up into Hades.
sm: and what had been given by God was being shut up into Hades.
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized as punctuation.

syn.mc: Main clause continuing the dissolution pair.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the participle δωρηθὲν modifies the neuter subject.
syn.svo: Subject: τὸ δωρηθέν. Verb: κατεκλείετο. Source phrase: ἐκ θεοῦ. Goal phrase: εἰς ἄδην.
syn.ptc: δωρηθὲν is passive and antecedent, highlighting divine gift.
syn.rhet: The human being is figured as simultaneously earthward and Hades-bound.

src: καὶ λύσις ἐγίνετο τῆς καλῆς ἁρμογῆς,
lit: And a loosening of the beautiful fitting was coming to be,
sm: And the beautiful fitting was coming apart,

syn.mc: Main clause summarizing the anthropological disintegration.
syn.svo: Subject: λύσις. Verb: ἐγίνετο. Genitive phrase: τῆς καλῆς ἁρμογῆς.
syn.rhet: The body is reimagined as a once-beautiful fitting now unraveling.

src: καὶ διεχωρίζετο τὸ καλὸν σῶμα.
lit: And the beautiful body was being separated.
sm: And the beautiful body was being separated.

syn.svo: Subject: τὸ καλὸν σῶμα. Verb: διεχωρίζετο.
syn.rhet: The theme of dismemberment is stated with tragic simplicity.

src: ἦν γὰρ ὁ ἄνθρωπος ὑπὸ τοῦ θανάτου μεριζόμενος.
tok: pres mp ptcp nom sg m
lit: For man was being divided by death.
sm: For man was being divided by death.

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; γάρ explains the preceding dissolution and separation.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ ἄνθρωπος. Verb: ἦν with participial complement μεριζόμενος. Agent phrase: ὑπὸ τοῦ θανάτου.
syn.ptc: μεριζόμενος is complementary to ἦν, expressing ongoing process.
syn.rhet: The anthropological crisis is generalized as the condition of man.

src: καινὴ γὰρ συμφορὰ καὶ ἅλωσις περιεῖχεν αὐτόν,
lit: For a new misfortune and capture was encompassing him,
sm: For a new misfortune and captivity was encompassing him,

syn.svo: Subject: καινὴ συμφορὰ καὶ ἅλωσις. Verb: περιεῖχεν. Object: αὐτόν.
syn.rhet: The affliction is newly intensified, not merely repeated.

src: καὶ εἵλκετο αἰχμάλωτος ὑπὸ τὰς τοῦ θανάτου σκιάς,
lit: And he was being dragged captive under the shadows of death,
sm: and he was being dragged away captive under the shadows of death,

syn.svo: Subject: implied ὁ ἄνθρωπος. Verb: εἵλκετο. Predicate noun: αἰχμάλωτος. Prepositional phrase: ὑπὸ τὰς ... σκιάς.
syn.rhet: The image moves from division to captivity.

src: ἔκειτο δὲ ἔρημος ἡ τοῦ πατρὸς εἰκών.
lit: And the image of the father lay desolate.
sm: And the image of the father lay desolate.

syn.svo: Subject: ἡ τοῦ πατρὸς εἰκών. Verb: ἔκειτο. Predicate adjective: ἔρημος.
syn.rhet: The ruined human state culminates in desolation of the paternal image.

src: διὰ ταύτην οὖν τὴν αἰτίαν τὸ τοῦ πάσχα μυστήριον
lit: Therefore because of this cause the mystery of the Passover
sm: Therefore for this reason the mystery of the Passover

syn.svo: No verb in this line; the phrase is causal and anticipatory.
syn.rhet: The exposition pivots to the theological conclusion.

src: τετέλεσται ἐν τῷ τοῦ κυρίου σώματι.
lit: has been completed in the body of the Lord.
sm: has been fulfilled in the body of the Lord.

syn.mc: Main clause completing the causal statement of line 424.
syn.svo: Subject: τὸ τοῦ πάσχα μυστήριον. Verb: τετέλεσται. Locative phrase: ἐν τῷ τοῦ κυρίου σώματι.
syn.rhet: The doctrinal conclusion is explicit: the Passover mystery is fulfilled in Christ's body.

src: Πρότερον δὲ ὃ κύριος προῳκονόμησεν τὰ ἑαυτοῦ πάθη
ana: Πρότερον δὲ ὁ κύριος προῳκονόμησεν τὰ ἑαυτοῦ πάθη
lit: Previously the Lord had prearranged his own sufferings
sm: Previously the Lord had prearranged his own sufferings
emd: Followed the article-vs-relative correction pattern used throughout the corpus: ὃ is read as ὁ here for the required subject article.

syn.mc: Main clause starting a retrospective statement.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the object phrase is completed by lines 428-429.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ κύριος. Verb: προῳκονόμησεν. Object: τὰ ἑαυτοῦ πάθη.
syn.rhet: The section turns from fulfillment back to divine prearrangement.

src: ἐν πατριάρχαις καὶ ἐν προφήταις καὶ ἐν παντὶ τῷ λαῷ,
lit: In the patriarchs and in the prophets and in all the people,
sm: in the patriarchs, the prophets, and all the people,

syn.svo: Locative series: ἐν πατριάρχαις, ἐν προφήταις, ἐν παντὶ τῷ λαῷ.
syn.rhet: Triadic historical breadth situates Christ's sufferings throughout salvation history.

src: διά τε νόμου καὶ προφητῶν ἐπισφραγισάμενος.
tok: aor mid ptcp nom sg m
lit: Having ratified through law and prophets.
sm: having ratified it through law and prophets.

syn.mc: Participial closure to the retrospective statement begun in line 427.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ κύριος. Participle: ἐπισφραγισάμενος. Means phrase: διά τε νόμου καὶ προφητῶν.
syn.ptc: ἐπισφραγισάμενος is antecedent/circumstantial, sealing the prior line's claim.
syn.rhet: The law and prophets are presented as divine ratification of the hidden passion.

src: τὸ γὰρ μέλλον καινῶς καὶ μεγάλως ἔσεσθαι,
tok: pres act ptcp nom sg n
lit: For the thing about to happen [is] to be newly and greatly,
sm: For what is to come is to be new and great,

syn.mc: Introductory explanatory clause, continued in the next line.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the infinitive ἔσεσθαι completes the predicate idea and lines 431ff will finish the thought.
syn.svo: Subject: τὸ μέλλον. Verb: ἔσεσθαι. Adverbials: καινῶς καὶ μεγάλως.
syn.ptc: μέλλον is substantival, 'the thing about to happen'.
syn.rhet: The future event is framed as both novel and magnificent.
